name: Ishaan Jaff
bio: Ishaan Jaff is the Co-Founder and CTO of LiteLLM (YC W23), an open-source LLM gateway that provides a unified OpenAI-compatible API for calling 100+ large language model providers. A Carnegie Mellon University graduate with a BS in Electrical and Computer Engineering and a minor in Machine Learning, Jaff previously held engineering roles at Coinbase, Qualcomm, and Credit Suisse. He co-founded BerriAI with Krrish Dholakia in 2023 after encountering the fragmentation problem of managing multiple LLM API providers first-hand. LiteLLM has grown to 39,000+ GitHub stars, 240M+ Docker pulls, and $7M ARR, serving enterprise customers including Adobe, NASA, Netflix, Stripe, and Nvidia.

timeline.event: BerriAI participates in Y Combinator W23 batch; raises $1.6M seed from YC, Gravity Fund, and Pioneer Fund
Co-founded BerriAI with Krrish Dholakia to solve LLM API fragmentation across providers
Initial release of LiteLLM open-source SDK for unified LLM API calls
BerriAI reaches $2.5M annual recurring revenue; launches Enterprise tiers (Basic at $250/mo, Premium at $30K/yr)
LiteLLM v1.17.0 optimizes Proxy cold boot times, adds gunicorn workers for Kubernetes/distributed systems
LiteLLM v1.41.5 adds guardrail configuration UI for the Proxy, enabling per-call guardrail control
LiteLLM v1.74.10 introduces per-tool consumption tracking and daily active users metrics
LiteLLM MCP protocol support launched (v1.80.18), cutting p99 latency by 70% on AI Gateway
LiteLLM adds BYOM (Bring Your Own MCP) with non-admin MCP server submission and admin review workflow
LiteLLM introduces Agent Hub: register, publish, and share agents via the AI Gateway (v1.80.0-stable)
LiteLLM v1.82 released with MCP OAuth2 support, Anthropic Files API integration, and performance improvements (2.9x lower median latency at 1K concurrent users)

key_contributions.title: LiteLLM
key_contributions.description: Open-source Python SDK and AI Gateway (Proxy Server) providing a unified OpenAI-compatible interface for 100+ LLM providers with cost tracking, guardrails, load balancing, and logging. 39,000+ GitHub stars, 240M+ Docker pulls, 6,500+ forks.

key_contributions.title: LiteLLM Proxy Server
key_contributions.description: FastAPI-based LLM proxy gateway enabling model fallback, virtual key management, spend tracking, SSO/SCIM/JWT auth, and web UI configuration without code redeployment.

key_contributions.title: LiteLLM Agent Hub
key_contributions.description: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) gateway for registering, publishing, and invoking agents through the AI Gateway with request/response logging and access controls.

key_contributions.title: LiteLLM MCP Integration
key_contributions.description: Model Context Protocol support on chat completions endpoint, enabling direct MCP server usage with 70% p99 latency reduction and BYOM (Bring Your Own MCP) admin workflows.

key_contributions.title: ReliableGPT
key_contributions.description: Early open-source tool for uptime and reliability handling of OpenAI API calls, providing automatic retries and fallback logic.

key_contributions.title: Clerkie-CLI
key_contributions.description: LLM-powered debugging tool for multiple programming languages, using AI to diagnose and explain code errors from the command line.

quotes.text: Calling LLM APIs involved multiple ~100 line if/else statements which made our debugging problems explode... provider-specific logic meant our code became increasingly complex and hard to debug. That's when we decided to abstract our LLM calls behind a single package - LiteLLM.
We needed I/O that just worked, so we could spend time improving other parts of our system - error-handling, model-fallback logic, and more.

sources.title: Unveiling BerriAI: Revolutionizing AI Debugging with LiteLLM - HireTop
